name: Taurage County (Lithuania)

colors.symbolism: the standard outer field of Lithuanian county flags
the silver field of the central coat of arms
gold details of the central coat of arms
details of the central coat of arms

description: A blue field with the coat of arms of Taurage County in the centre, set within an inner panel framed by the standard Lithuanian-county blue border.
history: Like the flags of all ten Lithuanian counties, Taurage County's flag follows the uniform template introduced after the country's redivision into apskritys (counties) in 1994: a blue field surrounding the county's heraldic device.

features.symbolism: blue outer border in the Lithuanian county pattern, surrounding the county's heraldic field

features.role: coat of arms of Taurage County
features.symbolism: the county's heraldic device, including emblems referencing Taurage's regional and historical character
